title: Fermented Tender Kenyan Beef Stir-fry
description: This low-fat-friendly Kenyan dish features tender minced beef mixed with fermented vegetables and pickled elements for a unique twist on traditional dishes.

ingredients.name: Pickled Vegetables (e.g. Paprika Pickle or Okra Pickle), finely chopped

instructions: Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and onion, cooking until translucent. Do not burn them.
Fold in the finely chopped fermented vegetables and pickled vegetables into the mixture.
Next, mix in the tomato paste, low-sodium soy sauce, and sugar syrup (or stevia), stirring well to ensure all ingredients are evenly coated.
Add pepper according to your taste preference. Mix everything together thoroughly, ensuring no lumps remain.
Gently fold in finely chopped Capsicum (Red Pepper) into the stir-fry mixture.
Cook for about 5 minutes until it starts to thicken and the vegetables start to soften.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for another 2-3 minutes, allowing flavors to meld together.
Turn off the heat immediately after removing from the heat. Serve hot with your favorite side dishes.
